name: Experian
description_short: One of the three major credit bureaus. Free FICO score, Experian Boost, dark web monitoring, and paid credit monitoring plans.
description_long:
Experian is one of the three major credit bureaus in the United States, alongside Equifax and TransUnion. Founded in 1996 and headquartered in Dublin, Ireland (US operations in Costa Mesa, CA), Experian maintains credit files on over 220 million consumers and 25 million businesses in the US.

Experian offers a free tier that includes your Experian FICO Score 8 (updated monthly), Experian credit report, basic credit monitoring, and dark web surveillance. Their paid CreditWorks Premium plan ($24.99/month) adds daily FICO score updates, 3-bureau credit monitoring, score tracking across all three bureaus, identity theft insurance up to $1 million, and access to Experian Boost.

Experian Boost is a free feature that lets you add positive payment history from bills you already pay — utilities, phone, streaming services, rent — to your Experian credit file. On average, users see a 12-point FICO score increase immediately. This is one of the few legitimate instant score-boosting tools available.

Experian also offers credit lock (instantly freeze/unfreeze your Experian file), dispute tools for errors on your Experian report, and educational resources about credit scores and reports.
pros: Experian Boost can instantly raise score 12+ points
Direct access to your Experian credit file
cons: Free tier only monitors Experian (not all 3 bureaus)
best_for: Instantly boosting your credit score with bill payments
services: Experian Boost (add bill payments to credit file)
